title: Are US Corporate Profit Margins Too High?
text:
Are US Corporate Profit Margins Too High?

Tan Kai Xian

Gavekal Research | 22 Jul 2026

One of the biggest surprises in the US economy today is the resilience of corporate profit margins. Conventional economic theory would suggest that rising labor costs, stricter immigration policies, the shift from just-in-time to just-in-case supply chains and tariff-driven disruption to globalization should all be squeezing margins. Yet they remain remarkably resilient. Why is this the case?US corporate profit margins remain elevated by historical standards. In the first quarter of 2026, US after-tax non-financial margins were estimated at 7.6%, only just below the post-1949 high of 8.2% reached in the second quarter of 2021. The second-quarter earnings season has also begun strongly, according to FactSet data. Artificial intelligence enthusiasts would argue that this resilience reflects AI reducing the need for labor. That is likely part of the story, but probably not the whole story.
